steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The opening “With a blend of traditional design elements, such as arched Gothic ceilings, and modern ones, such as floor-to-ceiling windows” is a dependent introductory phrase. What follows is an independent clause: “transitional design splits the difference between old and new, a mixture that is increasingly seen in home interiors in the US.”

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content: A comma separates an introductory phrase from the independent clause that follows. Choice D uses a comma after “windows,” to properly mark this boundary and allow “transitional design splits...” to serve as the main clause. The semicolon in A incorrectly suggests two independent clauses, the em dash in B provides emphasis rather than structural separation, and the period in C breaks the thought into separate sentences. That matches choice D — D is correct.
